title: Prepend to a list instead of appending — tail recursion with accumulator
prompt: Rewrite this function to use prepend-plus-reverse instead of `acc ++ [x]`. Original: `def squared([]), do: []` / `def squared([h | t]), do: squared(t) ++ [h * h]`. Produce an equivalent function that is tail-recursive using an accumulator. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Squares do ... end` with `squared/1` and a private helper.
tier_rationale: Canonical Elixir recursion idiom (prepend + reverse). Single transformation.
research_citation: georgeguimaraes elixir-thinking: 'Prepend to lists [new | list] not list ++ [new]'
